summary: Implement M4 march execution, supply state, and reinforcement timing
acceptance_criteria: Campaign forces can execute deterministic daily march steps from an accepted route plan, with authoritative position/progress held only in sim-core state.
Supply state updates daily from carried grain, route capacity, and force size, producing explicit well-supplied, strained, hungry, out-of-supply, delayed, and cancelled/paused reason codes.
Reinforcement timing supports promised detachments joining late, failing to arrive, or synchronizing arrival without duplicating mobilized commitments.
March outcomes can forecast and then record predicted versus actual arrival windows for player and AI reason display.
The task does not implement siege decisions, automatic battle resolution, postwar governance, campaign AI policy, or client UI behavior.
required_tests: Scenario tests cover synchronized arrival, late reinforcement arrival, supply shortage delay, and rainy-season cancellation or pause.
Property/invariant tests cover no negative supply, no duplicate reinforcement joins, deterministic daily march ordering, and shared player/AI command paths.
node -e JSON parse/shape check for M4-MARCH-SUPPLY-STATE-001
notes: March execution must stay deterministic and headless; UI/renderer must observe read models only.
Keep reinforcement timing as campaign commitments joining an existing plan, not a full army organization system.

Explicit exclusions for M4 remain: manual node battle, complex naval warfare, full diplomacy, 3D battle, multiplayer/server, telemetry, arbitrary-code mods, and frozen product or architecture changes.
